_comment: MODELED scenario (not a measured run). FEMA Public Assistance project-worksheet package review. Assumptions are transparent and documented in README.md. Replace with measured values from a real pilot to make this a primary-source case study.
task_description: Initial eligibility + documentation review of one FEMA Public Assistance project worksheet package (~150 pages: invoices, contracts, damage descriptions, force-account labor records, photos)

value._comment: Deliverable value = program value of one defensible, audit-ready review. AI version slightly higher due to more consistent documentation-completeness checking. See README sensitivity note — headline metrics are robust to this anchor.

cost_baseline._comment: Human reviewer at fully-loaded public-sector/contractor rate. Risk = expected cost of a downstream error (missed ineligible cost or doc gap triggering later deobligation/OIG finding): ~8% x $5,000.

cost_ai._comment: AI ingests the package, extracts cost line items, checks completeness against an eligibility checklist, drafts the determination narrative; human verifies. Residual risk ~4% x $5,000 (lower: AI catches doc gaps humans skim, but adds hallucination risk offset by verification).

validation._comment: Reviewer accepts ~88% of AI determinations/flags as-is; 12% modified. All reviewed packages are submitted.

overuse_benchmark.benchmark_description: Cheaper extraction model + tighter retrieval reaches comparable review quality at M=$1.20

notes: Modeled from plausible PA review economics. NOT a measured pilot. The point is to demonstrate the evaluator on a high-value public-sector workflow and to expose which assumptions matter.
